channel.title: Indian Defence Research Wing
channel.description: INDIAN DEFENCE NEWS, LATEST DEFENCE NEWS, INDIAN ARMY, INDIAN AIR FORCE, INDIAN NAVY, DEFENSE NEWS

item.title: From Bullet to Battery: Indian Army to Induct Silent Scout Bikes for Covert Missions
item.description: The Indian Army’s shift toward electric mobility reached a defining moment in March 2026 with the issuance and closure of key tenders aimed at replacing traditional internal combustion engine motorcycles with a new class of “Silent Scout” electric two-wheelers. These platforms are not merely a logistical upgrade but represent a doctrinal shift in how mobility, reconnaissance, and communication are executed in forward areas.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: SSS Defence Showcases Indigenous 5R Rifling Barrels, Claims Performance Edge Over Foreign Small Arms
item.description: Indian small arms manufacturer SSS Defence has drawn attention within defence circles after unveiling details of its in-house developed “5R rifling” barrel technology, positioning it as a major step toward indigenous superiority in precision weapon systems. In a detailed post on X, the company highlighted both the engineering philosophy behind the design and the challenges it overcame to industrialise the technology within India’s evolving defence manufacturing ecosystem.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: TridenTech Successfully Tests Indigenous 30 HP Wankel Engine at NAL, Secures CEMILAC Approval for Military UAV Applications
item.description: In a notable advancement for India’s indigenous aerospace propulsion ecosystem, Chennai-based TridenTech Engineering Pvt Ltd has successfully completed ground testing of its 30 HP Wankel rotary engine at the National Aerospace Laboratories (NAL) Belur campus in Bengaluru. The engine, developed under a collaborative production partnership with NAL, has also received certification clearance from CEMILAC (Centre for Military Airworthiness and Certification), marking its readiness for deployment in military-grade unmanned aerial systems.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Operation Abhinandan: How India Used Coercive Diplomacy to Secure Its Pilot’s Return from Pakistan in Just 60 Hours
item.description: In the aftermath of the February 2019 India–Pakistan aerial confrontation, the capture and rapid return of Indian Air Force pilot Wing Commander Abhinandan Varthaman emerged as a defining case study in modern coercive diplomacy. While contemporary narratives often highlight kinetic military operations such as combat search-and-rescue missions, India’s approach demonstrated how calibrated escalation, diplomatic signaling, and international pressure can achieve strategic objectives without crossing the threshold into full-scale conflict.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India’s Fast Breeder Reactor Breakthrough: How Kalpakkam’s PFBR Signals the Start of a 700-Year Thorium Energy Strategy
item.description: India’s long-delayed Prototype Fast Breeder Reactor (PFBR) at Kalpakkam achieving criticality marks a structural inflection point in global nuclear energy strategy. After decades of technical setbacks, cost overruns, and international skepticism toward fast breeder technology, India has moved into the second stage of its uniquely designed three-stage nuclear program—an approach conceived not out of ambition alone, but out of necessity.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Pakistan’s Own R&D Wing Admits 12-Year Lag Behind India in Aerospace Development Ecosystem
item.description: A confidential study conducted by Pakistan Air Force’s premier research and development organisation, the National Aerospace Science and Technology Park (NASTP), has reportedly acknowledged that Pakistan trails India by approximately 12 years in building a comprehensive aerospace development ecosystem. This internal assessment from Pakistan’s own experts highlights the widening capability gap in indigenous design, testing, certification, and production of advanced aerial platforms, sensors, and related technologies.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Sagar Defence Engineering Secures Explosives Manufacturing License, Accelerates India’s Push for Indigenous Full-Spectrum Defence Capabilities
item.description: Sagar Defence Engineering (SDE) has taken a decisive step toward transforming itself into a fully integrated defence solutions provider, following the grant of an industrial license for the manufacturing of explosives. This development positions the company within a highly strategic segment of the defence industrial ecosystem, enabling it to vertically integrate critical capabilities required for next-generation warfare systems.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: GE officials in India to discuss F-404 and F-414 engines
item.description: A high-level delegation from GE Aerospace, led by Rita Flaherty, VP Sales & Business Development, visited Hindustan Aeronautics Limited in Bengaluru today. The team met Dr. D K Sunil, CMD, HAL, Shri Ravi K, Director (Operations) & senior leadership to discuss progress on the GE F404 engines & ongoing discussions on the GE F414 programme.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Discussed ways to build strong future of India-U.S. ties: U.S. envoy Gor on meeting with Trump
item.description: US Ambassador to India, Sergio Gor who is in the United States attended a dinner at the White House on Tuesday night and said that during the dinner he discussed US-India ties with President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India set to receive first Iranian oil cargo in 7 years: Report
item.description: India is set to receive its first oil from ?Iran in seven years after the US ?temporarily removed sanctions on Iranian oil and refined products ?to ease supply shortages, ship tracking data from LSEG and Kpler showed on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: US ‘Forced’ Into Ceasefire, Says Iranian Supreme Leader’s India Representative
item.description: The Iranian Supreme Leader’s representative in India, Abdul Majid Hakeem Ilahi, on Wednesday claimed that the United States was compelled to announce a ceasefire with Iran after failing to sustain the conflict, and asserting that continued hostilities would have led to further setbacks for Washington.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: ‘No Labels, Just Jihad’: Inside The Rise Of Hybrid Terror Modules In India
item.description: The recent busting of several terror modules created to strike in India has revealed a new pattern. There is not one single group that the members of these modules are attached to. The Faridabad module had members who were part of the Jaish-e-Mohammad. However, the ideology that they largely subscribed to was that of the Islamic State.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India Welcomes US-Iran Ceasefire, Hopes Agreement ‘Will Lead To Lasting Peace’
item.description: India on Wednesday welcomed the ceasefire agreement reached between the US and Iran and expressed hope that the ceasefire would lead to lasting peace in West Asia.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Celebrations across Kashmir after US-Iran ceasefire announcement
item.description: Celebrations erupted in various places across Kashmir after the US-Iran ceasefire announcement, with people calling it a “victory” for the Islamic Republic.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India Asks Its Nationals In Iran To ‘Expeditiously Exit’
item.description: India on Wednesday advised its nationals in Iran to “expeditiously exit” the country using routes suggested by its mission in Tehran in light of the recent developments. In an advisory, the Indian embassy in Tehran also reiterated that there should be “no attempt to approach any international land border without prior consultation and coordination with the Embassy.”
item.dc:creator: admin
